mnemonics: A.C.I.D. for Recoverable Schedules: Always Commit In Dependency order. A schedule is recoverable if a transaction only commits after all the transactions it depends on (that wrote data it read) have committed.
keyPoints: A recoverable schedule ensures no committed transaction reads data from an aborted transaction, preventing dirty reads.
Cascadeless schedules (or avoiding cascading rollbacks) are stricter, requiring transactions to only read from committed transactions, preventing a chain of rollbacks.
Strict schedules are the strictest, requiring transactions to read and write only from committed transactions, simplifying recovery by allowing undo operations only on the last transaction.
commonMistakes: Assuming that a serializable schedule is automatically recoverable; while serializability ensures consistency, it does not guarantee recoverability on its own.
Confusing cascadeless with strict schedules; all strict schedules are cascadeless, but not all cascadeless schedules are strict (the difference is in the write rule).
